dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'M GRUMPY TODAY, SO DON'T EVEN TRY TO TALK TO ME.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: AND DON'T TRY TO FLATTER ME. I'D GIVE YOU CHOCOLATE CAKE TO MAKE ME FEEL BETTER.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: AND I GUESS I SHOULD THE SCRAIT YOU BRING THE EARS UNTIL YOU HAVE LITTLE LEG SPAMS. RIGHT, NONE OF THAT.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'M STARTING TO WRITE AN AMAZED BIOGRAPHY ABOUT YOU.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: IT'S KIND OF A 'PET' FULL OF STARTLING REVELATIONS.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: WHO WOULD BE STARTLED BY MY LIFE?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Are you really going through with the unauthorized photography of me?

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: I'm up to the part where Jackle ' and Liz Taylor fight a duel for your love.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Tragically, neither are aware that you're carrying Steve Garvey's baby!

dialogue.speaker: Background Character
dialogue.text: Welcome to another meeting of the Skeptics Society.

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: Tonight we will use scientific methods to debunk Edna's claim that she can turn an audience into a flock of chickens.

dialogue.speaker: Dilbert
dialogue.text: We'll need some volunteers.

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: Motion to adjourn... who, look at the time!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: GOOD NEWS! THE 'FULL YOU CAN EAT' SAUD BAR JUST DECIDED TO STAY OPEN TWENTY FOUR HOURS A DAY!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: WE CAN GET A TABLE BY THE WINDOW AND LIVE THERE FOR THE REST OF OUR LIVES - FOR ONLY $5.45 A PLEEE!

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: HOW WOULD WE BRIEVE?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: THEY HAVE LITTLE MOST TUMBLETS.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: DO YOU HAVE SOMETHING FOR A HEADACHE?

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: I'M PRETTY SURE THIS WILL DO THE TRICK.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: THANKS.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: I WONDER IF HE MEANT SOME RUD OF A HEADACHE.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: NAH.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Bob's Classy Clothes

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: AND YOU'RE QUITE CERTAIN THAT THESE WILL SHRINK TO FIT?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: YOU HAVE MY WRITTEN PLAN, SIR.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: WHERE ARE THEY? I'D LIKE TO SEE WHAT'S IN THE WASH.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: DOH! THEY'RE IN THE DRYER!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: LIKE A GLUE FINGER WITH IT TIGHT NOW?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I've got to get out of this dad mood somehow.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: I'll have to do a landmine... and make him plead for my retrieve-ness.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Hi, Dogbert. Is that some kind of an insult?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I CAN FEEL THE STATIC ELECTRICITY BUILDING...

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: SPLAT!

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: SPLAT!

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: SPLAT!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I POST CERTAINLY WILL NOT GAIN YOU 'THOR' DOGS OF THUNDER, OR PREPARE TO DIE.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Maybe since you're full of static electricity you should say "It is useless to be a resistor. Hee-hee.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: ZAP!

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: NOTHING ANNOYS THE DOG OF THUNDER QUITE AS MUCH AS MERO PUNS.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Let's go... it's time to renew your dog's license.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: I wonder what happens if I fail the written test.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: It's sorry, but it seems you've failed the written portion of the dog license test.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: For example, this question on the correct answer is 'Mailman.' You wrote 'IN FAX MACHINE'.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: How'd it go?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: The department doesn't keep up with emerging trends.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I think it's my fuel pump.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: Your what?

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: What I mean is I think it's my fuel pump.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: Well, maybe I didn't say so clearly.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Sorry... I forgot where I was.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: You know man, many green men kept DILLERS.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Not to mention the entire fan club.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Well, this was both therapeutic and satisfying.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Sometimes it's good to break your own lease, man.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Who the heck is Captain Pudding?

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: You what?!

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: I got a job as a substitute school teacher.

dialogue.speaker: Dilbert
dialogue.text: You aren't qualified to teach. You're a dog.

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: Little kids won't know the difference.

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: You do re-learn me a bit of my fourth grade teacher.

dialogue.speaker: Dilbert
dialogue.text: Just a coincidence?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Can't I talk you out of becoming a sub-stitute teacher?

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Don't worry.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: I won't damage the little tykes.

dialogue.speaker: CHILDREN
dialogue.text: Good morning, Mr. Dogbert!

dialogue.speaker: CHILDREN
dialogue.text: Are you flammable?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: DAY ONE AS A SUBSTITUTE TEACHER. JENNER! PUT THAT FLAME-THROWER AWAY RIGHT THIS MINUTE!

dialogue.speaker: EUGENE
dialogue.text: EUGENE! RELEASE THESE HOSTAGES OR I SHALL BE FORCED TO FLING THIS CHALK ERASER AT YOUR HEAD!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: IS THAT A 'STUNGER' MISSILE? I BROUGHT ENOUGH FOR EVERYBODY! I DID.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Okay, class... put your weapons away and open your TV guides.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Tinytiny please read from 'palcon creest' under the friday listings.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: There's got to be a better way to teach sex education.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: What was your first day as a substitute school teacher?

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Imagine feeling completely powerless... like a marble statue.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Gosh... that sounds pretty bad. Now imagine the biggest flock of prisoners you ever saw.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I think I'm losing my hair.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Don't be silly. You aren't losing your hair.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm not? Oh, cool.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: How could you possibly lose these clumps...

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Kamikaze

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: All I'm asking is their protection of ugly people.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: We are dedicated to eliminating the stupid as 'sport' and 'nice.'

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Okay, call me a donation.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Thanks, but this is a resentment issue.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I've never going to door main.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: Didn't he recognize you as his god?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: ... AND KNEWEN HAVE ALWAYS PLAYED HARD TO GET...

dialogue.speaker: MAN IN WHITE SHIRT
dialogue.text: ... THEN HOW ABOUT A DATE NEXT YEAR?

dialogue.speaker: DOG
dialogue.text: TO LOVE TO, BUT I DON'T RAVE A THING TO WEAR.

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: Normally I'd give you six months to live.

dialogue.speaker: Dilbert
dialogue.text: But we're having a '50% off sale' today. So I'll give you a whole extra ten days in the same price!

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: And you get an extra ten days if you fry doom!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: ...AND THIS DOCTOR GIVE ME JUST A YEAR TO LIVE.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: I'M SORRY, LITTLE GUY... I DON'T KNOW HOW YOU'LL MANAGE WITHOUT ME.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: WOULD IT BE TOO MUCH TROUBLE TO PAINT THE HOUSE BEFORE YOU GO?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It seems we had a mix up with your test results. Then I'm not drinking.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: We doctors are amazingly smart, but occasionally we make a little error.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Well... I understand.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: By the way, your pen was normal.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: BY MY CALCULATIONS WE CAN MAKE MILLIONS BY COMBINING A PORKY BUSINESS AND A GARBAGE COLLECTION BUSINESS

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: OUR CUSTOMERS COULD SIMPLY LEAVE THE DEARLY DEPRESSED BY THE CURB FOR PICKUP

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: MAYBE WE COULD ADD PIZZA DELIVERY TOO.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: LET'S NOT PUSH IT ANY FURTHER. I'DEN TO YOU PARK

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I HAVE FANCY HOTELS LIKE THE WELLS.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Ah! I EXPECTED TO SEE A TIP IN THE MAIL. $394

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: WHERE'S MY TIP, DOGBERT?

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: I COULD ASK A GIRL WITH A FANCY HOTEL FOR A TIP. CAN I TRUST A GUY WITH A FANCY HOTEL?

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: AND DO I HAVE TO TIP HIM?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I HAVE TO TIP HIM! HMMM.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I've got a script to conquer empa track.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: You been applying for credit cards?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'll use this credit history to apply for a loan.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: Some guy home credit card every bank in the world.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Then I'll do a cash advance on every card, netting billions to toy world white lottery prize.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: And everybody who supports me as faith gets one free lottery ticket.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: You know, most people believe just to get a nice clean toy.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'VE DECIDED TO WRITE DOWN ALL OF THE SO-CALLED RULES.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: SO FAR I HAVE 'DON'T PHONE AFTER TEN P.M.' AND... UN.

dialogue.speaker: DOG BERT
dialogue.text: THAT'S IT?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: HOW ABOUT 'DON'T THROW PORCUPINES IN A BALLOON FROM THE STORE'?

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: While you were waiting your time at work I came up with a million dollar idea.

dialogue.speaker: Dilbert
dialogue.text: This the 'Madonna applicator' for lonely guys!!

dialogue.speaker: Dogbert
dialogue.text: It looks like a barbeque on a stick, but it's Madonna.

dialogue.speaker: Dilbert
dialogue.text: I'll take one.
